cognitive_layer.purpose: UMAP is a dimensionality reduction technique that preserves both local and some global structure. It provides excellent 2D visualization of single-cell data, revealing relationships between cell populations that appear as distinct clusters or trajectories.

parameter_impact.min_dist: Controls how tightly UMAP packs points together. Lower values create tighter clusters; higher values create more diffuse embeddings. 0.5 is a good balance.
parameter_impact.n_neighbors: Number of neighbors used for UMAP. More neighbors considers more global structure. Fewer neighbors focuses on local relationships.

error_handling.MemoryError: For large datasets, consider computing UMAP on a subsample first.

overlapping_clusters.causal_chain: Lower min_dist creates tighter, more distinct clusters by allowing UMAP to pack points closer together in the embedding.

fragmented_structure.causal_chain: Higher min_dist forces UMAP to spread points apart more evenly, reducing tight packing and potentially revealing continuous trajectories.
